Test drain basic behaviour, for more 'details' see the test-case-design doc
Utility)
testbasec
	TestDrainc
superr
myinitZ
_start_ovsZ
_start_netagent)
self
	__class__
:/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/na_tests/ft/test_drain.pyr
TestDrain.myinitc
cd {test_dir}
ovs-vsctl add-br br0 -- set bridge br0 fail-mode=standalone --     add-port br0 p1 -- set interface p1 ofport_request=1 type=dummy --     add-port br0 p2 -- set interface p2 ofport_request=2 type=dummy --     add-port br0 p3 -- set interface p3 ofport_request=3 type=dummy --     add-port br0 p4 -- set interface p4 ofport_request=4 type=dummy --     add-port br0 phy1 -- set interface phy1 ofport_request=5 type=dummy
ovs-vsctl set interface p1 external_ids:attached-mac=50:54:00:00:00:0a --     set interface p2 external_ids:attached-mac=50:54:00:00:00:0b --     set interface p3 external_ids:attached-mac=50:54:00:00:00:0c --     set interface p4 external_ids:attached-mac=50:54:00:00:00:0d
ovs-vsctl add-br br1 -- set bridge br1 fail-mode=standalone
ovs-vsctl add-br brout -- set bridge brout fail-mode=standalone
# connect br1 with brout
ovs-vsctl add-port br1 phy2 -- set interface phy2 type=patch options:peer=phy3     -- add-port brout phy3 -- set interface phy3 type=patch options:peer=phy2
ovs-ofctl mod-port br0 br0 noforward
vlan1=10
vlan2=20
add_out_flow() {{
    ovs-ofctl -t 5 -O OpenFlow13 --bundle add-flows brout - <<EOF
cookie=0x70,in_port=phy3,priority=40000,dl_vlan=$2,actions=pop_vlan,push_vlan:0x8100,set_vlan_vid:$1,IN_PORT
cookie=0x70,in_port=phy3,priority=40000,dl_vlan=$1,actions=pop_vlan,push_vlan:0x8100,set_vlan_vid:$2,IN_PORT
add_out_flow $vlan1 $vlan2
get_net_config() {{
    cat <<EOF
<netprofile type='drain' name='drain_netprofile' uuid='b71cec3c-2b97-4de8-ac68-ba3f3067b3aa' version='1'>
  <flow direction='in' priority='0' dl_type='ip'>
    <action bridge="br1" vlan="$vlan1" />
  </flow>
  <flow direction='out' priority='0' dl_type='ip'>
    <action bridge="br1" vlan="$vlan2" />
  </flow>
</netprofile>
na-ctl --retry=-1 netprofile define --content "$(get_net_config)"
get_port_config() {{
    cat <<EOF
<portprofile name="port_config" type="compose">
  <netprofile name="drain_netprofile" type="drain" />
</portprofile>
# emulate libvirt to deliver according to the domain xml>interface
ovs-appctl revalidator/purge
na-ctl --retry=-1 portprofile define --content "$(get_port_config)"
na-ctl add-listen --id=1 --event="set Interface p1 external_ids:port-profile=port_config"
ovs-vsctl set Interface p1 external_ids:port-profile=port_config
na-ctl wait --id=1
exitret=0
if ! ovs-appctl show-internal-version | grep -q 'feature-loutput'; then
    echo "ovs is not built with loutput feature, validation already fails, but continue to test other"
    exitret=88
if ! ovs-appctl dpif/show | grep pe_br1_br0; then
    exit 2 # patch port not exit
ovs-ofctl -O OpenFlow13 --no-stats dump-flows br0 table=15 > br0_flows.txt
grep '50:54:00:00:00:0a' br0_flows.txt
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 3 # not expected flows
ovs-appctl revalidator/purge
# mac learning
ovs-appctl ofproto/trace "in_port(1),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff)" -generate
ovs-appctl ofproto/trace "in_port(2),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0b,dst=ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff)" -generate
ovs-appctl ofproto/trace "in_port(3),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0c,dst=ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff)" -generate
ovs-appctl ofproto/trace "in_port(4),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0d,dst=ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff)" -generate
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p1 'in_port(1),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:05,dst=50:54:00:00:01:00),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=1.2.3.6,dst=1.2.3.5,proto=6,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),tcp(src=8,dst=7),tcp_flags(ack)'
ovs-appctl time/warp 100
ovs-appctl dpif/dump-flows br0 > br0_flows3.txt
grep 'actions:2' br0_flows3.txt
ovs-appctl revalidator/purge
na-ctl add-listen --id=2 --event="remove Interface p1 external_ids:port-profile"
ovs-vsctl remove Interface p1 external_ids port-profile
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 33
na-ctl wait --id=2  # so net-agentd knows the configuration
ovs-ofctl dump-flows br0 > br0_flows1.txt
if grep -q '50:54:00:00:00:0a' br0_flows1.txt; then
    exit 4 # has extra flows
# now we still have portprofile, netprofile
if ! na-ctl portprofile dump | grep 'netprofile name="drain_netprofile"'; then
    exit 6
if ! na-ctl netprofile dump | grep 'drain_netprofile'; then
    exit 5
na-ctl netprofile undefine drain_netprofile
if na-ctl netprofile dump | grep 'drain_netprofile'; then
    exit 16
na-ctl portprofile undefine port_config
if na-ctl portprofile dump | grep 'netprofile name="drain_netprofile"'; then
    exit 15
# we can't grep na_test.log here as the script we log into this file
grep -qn '|ERR' cas_net_agentd.log ovsdb-server.log ovs-vswitchd.log && exit 11
grep -qn 'Traceback' cas_net_agentd.log && exit 10
exit $exitret  # this needed as some step may erroneously set $?
test_dirTr
z2test add basic config failed: shell return code:%d
cmd_execute
formatZ	_test_dirZ
assertEqual
retr
test_basic_config
TestDrain.test_basic_configc
cd {test_dir}
ovs-vsctl add-br br0 -- set bridge br0 fail-mode=standalone --     add-port br0 p1 -- set interface p1 ofport_request=1 type=dummy --     add-port br0 p2 -- set interface p2 ofport_request=2 type=dummy --     add-port br0 p3 -- set interface p3 ofport_request=3 type=dummy --     add-port br0 p4 -- set interface p4 ofport_request=4 type=dummy --     add-port br0 phy1 -- set interface phy1 ofport_request=5 type=dummy
ovs-vsctl add-br br1 -- set bridge br1 fail-mode=standalone
ovs-vsctl add-br brout -- set bridge brout fail-mode=standalone
ovs-vsctl set interface p1 external_ids:attached-mac=50:54:00:00:00:0a --     set interface p2 external_ids:attached-mac=50:54:00:00:00:0b --     set interface p3 external_ids:attached-mac=50:54:00:00:00:0c --     set interface p4 external_ids:attached-mac=50:54:00:00:00:0d
ovs-vsctl set Port p1 tag=5 --     set Port p2 tag=5 --     set Port p3 tag=5 --     set Port p4 tag=10
# connect br1 with brout
ovs-vsctl add-port br1 phy2 -- set interface phy2 type=patch options:peer=phy3     -- add-port brout phy3 -- set interface phy3 type=patch options:peer=phy2
ovs-ofctl mod-port br0 br0 noforward
add_out_flow() {{
    ovs-ofctl -t 5 -O OpenFlow13 --bundle add-flows brout - <<EOF
cookie=0x70,in_port=phy3,priority=40000,dl_vlan=$2,actions=pop_vlan,push_vlan:0x8100,set_vlan_vid:$1,IN_PORT
cookie=0x70,in_port=phy3,priority=40000,dl_vlan=$1,actions=pop_vlan,push_vlan:0x8100,set_vlan_vid:$2,IN_PORT
add_out_flow 10 20
NUMB=4
get_perm() {{
python3 - $NUMB <<EOF
#!/usr/bin/python3
import sys
from itertools import permutations
import random
b = range(int(sys.argv[1]))
for i in permutations(b, len(b)):
    lst = list(i)
    random.shuffle(lst)
    for i in lst:
        print(i)
    break
get_net_config() {{
    cat <<EOF
<netprofile type='drain' name="net_drain$3" uuid="b71cec3c-2b97-4de8-ac68-ba3f3067b3a$3" version='1'>
  <flow direction='in' priority='0' dl_type='ip'>
    <action bridge="br1" vlan="$1" />
  </flow>
  <flow direction='out' priority='0' dl_type='ip'>
    <action bridge="br1" vlan="$2" />
  </flow>
</netprofile>
get_port_config() {{
    cat <<EOF
<portprofile name="port_config$1" type="compose">
  <netprofile name="net_drain$1" type="drain" />
</portprofile>
get_vlans() {{
python3 - $1 <<EOF
#!/usr/bin/python3
import sys
vlan1 = [10, 30, 10, 40]
vlan2 = [20, 10, 20, 10]
i = int(sys.argv[1])
print("%d %d" % (vlan1[i], vlan2[i]))
py_add() {{
python3 - $1 $2 <<EOF
#!/usr/bin/python3
import sys
print("%d" % (int(sys.argv[1]) + int(sys.argv[2])))
#vlan1=(10 30 10 40)
#vlan2=(20 10 20 10)
for i in $(get_perm); do
    # not work on ubuntu (expr also not work, due to bad/old sh): ((I=i+1))
    I=$(py_add $i 1)
    # not work on ubuntu: na-ctl --retry=-1 netprofile define --content "$(get_net_config ${{vlan1[$i]}} ${{vlan2[$i]}} $i)"
    na-ctl --retry=-1 netprofile define --content "$(get_net_config $(get_vlans $i) $i)"
    # emulate libvirt to deliver according to the domain xml>interface
    ovs-appctl revalidator/purge
    na-ctl --retry=-1 portprofile define --content "$(get_port_config $i)"
    na-ctl add-listen --id=$I --event="set Interface p$I external_ids:port-profile=port_config$i"
    ovs-vsctl set Interface p$I external_ids:port-profile=port_config$i
    na-ctl wait --id=$I
done
for i in $(get_perm); do
    # not work on ubuntu: ((I=i+1))
    # not work on ubuntu: ((j=i+20))
    I=$(py_add $i 1)
    J=$(py_add $i 20)
    na-ctl add-listen --id=$j --event="remove Interface p$I external_ids:port-profile"
    ovs-vsctl remove Interface p$I external_ids port-profile
    if test $? -ne 0; then
        exit 30
    fi
    na-ctl wait --id=$j
done
exitret=0
if ! ovs-appctl show-internal-version | grep -q 'feature-loutput'; then
    echo "ovs is not built with loutput feature, validation already fails, but continue to test other"
    exitret=88
ovs-ofctl -O OpenFlow13 --no-stats dump-flows br0 table=15 > br0_flows.txt
if grep '50:54:00:00:00:0a' br0_flows.txt; then
    exit 21 # expected no this mac flows
if grep '50:54:00:00:00:0b' br0_flows.txt; then
    exit 22 # expected no this mac flows
if grep '50:54:00:00:00:0c' br0_flows.txt; then
    exit 23 # expected no this mac flows
if grep '50:54:00:00:00:0d' br0_flows.txt; then
    exit 24 # expected no this mac flows
ovs-ofctl -O OpenFlow13 --no-stats dump-flows br1 table=0 > br1_flows.txt
if grep '50:54:00:00:00:0a' br1_flows.txt; then
    exit 31 # expected no this mac flows
if grep '50:54:00:00:00:0b' br1_flows.txt; then
    exit 32 # expected no this mac flows
if grep '50:54:00:00:00:0c' br1_flows.txt; then
    exit 33 # expected no this mac flows
if grep '50:54:00:00:00:0d' br1_flows.txt; then
    exit 34 # expected no this mac flows
# now we still have portprofile, netprofile
if ! na-ctl portprofile dump | grep 'netprofile name="net_drain'; then
    exit 44
if ! na-ctl netprofile dump | grep 'net_drain'; then
    exit 45
for i in $(get_perm); do
    na-ctl netprofile undefine net_drain$i
done
if na-ctl netprofile dump | grep 'net_drain'; then
    exit 15
for i in $(get_perm); do
    na-ctl portprofile undefine port_config$i
done
if na-ctl portprofile dump | grep 'netprofile name="net_drain'; then
    exit 14
# we can't grep na_test.log here as the script we log into this file
grep -qn '|ERR' cas_net_agentd.log ovsdb-server.log ovs-vswitchd.log && exit 11
grep -qn 'Traceback' cas_net_agentd.log && exit 10
exit $exitret  # this needed as some step may erroneously set $?
z-test clean setup failed: shell return code:%dr
test_clean_setup
TestDrain.test_clean_setupc
cd {test_dir}
ovs-vsctl add-br br0 -- set bridge br0 fail-mode=standalone --     add-port br0 p1 -- set interface p1 ofport_request=1 type=dummy --     add-port br0 p2 -- set interface p2 ofport_request=2 type=dummy --     add-port br0 p3 -- set interface p3 ofport_request=3 type=dummy --     add-port br0 p4 -- set interface p4 ofport_request=4 type=dummy --     add-port br0 phy1 -- set interface phy1 ofport_request=5 type=dummy
ovs-vsctl set interface p1 external_ids:attached-mac=50:54:00:00:00:0a --     set interface p2 external_ids:attached-mac=50:54:00:00:00:0b --     set interface p3 external_ids:attached-mac=50:54:00:00:00:0c --     set interface p4 external_ids:attached-mac=50:54:00:00:00:0d
ovs-ofctl mod-port br0 br0 noforward
ovs-vsctl add-br br1 -- set bridge br1 fail-mode=standalone
ovs-vsctl add-br brout -- set bridge brout fail-mode=standalone
exitret=0
if ! ovs-appctl show-internal-version | grep -q 'feature-loutput'; then
    echo "ovs is not built with loutput feature, validation already fails, but continue to test other"
    exitret=88
get_net_config() {{
    cat <<EOF
<netprofile type='acl' name='net_config' uuid='809d3091-9724-4ead-acbd-24754aba63f7' version='1'>
  <flow direction='in' priority='4' stateful='true' dl_type='ip'>
    <action target='drop'/>
  </flow>
  <flow direction='out' priority='4' stateful='true' dl_type='ip'>
    <action target='drop'/>
  </flow>
  <flow direction='in' priority='4' stateful='true' dl_type='ipv6'>
    <action target='drop'/>
  </flow>
  <flow direction='out' priority='4' stateful='true' dl_type='ipv6'>
    <action target='drop'/>
  </flow>
  <flow direction='out' priority='10' stateful='true' dl_type='ip' nw_src='1.2.3.5/32' nw_proto='tcp'>
    <action target='accept'/>
  </flow>
</netprofile>
get_net_config1() {{
    cat <<EOF
<netprofile type='acl' name='net_config1' uuid='809d3091-9724-4ead-acbd-24754aba6321' version='1'>
  <flow direction='in' priority='4' stateful='true' dl_type='ip'>
    <action target='drop'/>
  </flow>
  <flow direction='out' priority='4' stateful='true' dl_type='ip'>
    <action target='drop'/>
  </flow>
  <flow direction='in' priority='4' stateful='true' dl_type='ipv6'>
    <action target='drop'/>
  </flow>
  <flow direction='out' priority='4' stateful='true' dl_type='ipv6'>
    <action target='drop'/>
  </flow>
  <flow direction='out' priority='10' stateful='true' dl_type='ip' nw_src='1.2.3.6/32' nw_proto='tcp'>
    <action target='accept'/>
  </flow>
</netprofile>
get_net_config2() {{
    cat <<EOF
<netprofile type='acl' name='net_config2' uuid='809d3091-9724-4ead-acbd-24754aba6322' version='1'>
  <flow direction='in' priority='4' stateful='false' dl_type='ip'>
    <action target='drop'/>
  </flow>
  <flow direction='out' priority='4' stateful='false' dl_type='ip'>
    <action target='drop'/>
  </flow>
  <flow direction='in' priority='4' stateful='false' dl_type='ipv6'>
    <action target='drop'/>
  </flow>
  <flow direction='out' priority='4' stateful='false' dl_type='ipv6'>
    <action target='drop'/>
  </flow>
  <flow direction='out' priority='10' stateful='false' dl_type='ip' nw_src='1.2.3.7/32' nw_proto='tcp'>
    <action target='accept'/>
  </flow>
</netprofile>
ADDR1=1.2.3.6
ADDRD=1.2.3.7
get_qos_config() {{
    cat <<EOF
<netprofile name="qos_config" type="qos" uuid="245324" version="1">
  <flow direction="in" dl_type="ip" nw_dst="$ADDR1/24" nw_proto="icmp" priority="15">
    <action burst="2" domain="interface" id="5" rate="1" unit="kbps" />
  </flow>
</netprofile>
get_drain_config() {{
    cat <<EOF
<netprofile type='drain' name='drain_config' uuid='b71cec3c-2b97-4de8-ac68-ba3f3067b3aa' version='1'>
  <flow direction='in' priority='0' dl_type='ip'>
    <action bridge="br1" vlan="10" />
  </flow>
  <flow direction='out' priority='0' dl_type='ip'>
    <action bridge="br1" vlan="20" />
  </flow>
</netprofile>
na-ctl --retry=-1 netprofile define --content "$(get_net_config)" || exit 21
na-ctl --retry=-1 netprofile define --content "$(get_net_config1)" || exit 22
na-ctl --retry=-1 netprofile define --content "$(get_net_config2)" || exit 23
na-ctl --retry=-1 netprofile define --content "$(get_qos_config)" || exit 24
na-ctl --retry=-1 netprofile define --content "$(get_drain_config)" || exit 25
get_port_config() {{
    cat <<EOF
<portprofile name="port_config" type="compose">
  <netprofile name="net_config" type="acl" />
  <netprofile name="net_config1" type="acl" />
  <netprofile name="net_config2" type="acl" />
  <netprofile name="qos_config" type="qos" />
  <netprofile name="drain_config" type="drain" />
</portprofile>
# emulate libvirt to deliver according to the domain xml>interface
ovs-appctl revalidator/purge
na-ctl --retry=-1 portprofile define --content "$(get_port_config)"
na-ctl add-listen --id=1 --event="set Interface p2 external_ids:port-profile=port_config"
ovs-vsctl set Interface p2 external_ids:port-profile=port_config
na-ctl wait --id=1
ovs-ofctl dump-flows br0 > br0_flows.txt
grep "ct_state=+new-est,tcp,reg1=0x.*/0xffff,nw_src=1.2.3.5" br0_flows.txt || exit 2
grep "ct_state=+new-est,tcp,reg1=0x.*/0xffff,nw_src=1.2.3.6" br0_flows.txt || exit 3
grep "tcp,reg1=0x.*/0xffff,nw_src=1.2.3.7" br0_flows.txt || exit 4
grep "ct_state=+new-est,tcp,reg1=0x.*/0xffff,nw_src=1.2.3.7" br0_flows.txt && exit 5  # intended for no ct_state here
# Strips out uninteresting parts of flow output, as well as parts
# that vary from one run to another (e.g., timing and bond actions).
strip_timers() {{
    sed '
    s/duration:[0-9]*\.[0-9]*/duration:0.0/
    s/used:[0-9]*\.[0-9]*/used:0.0/
ovs-appctl time/warp 5000
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p2 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p2 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p2 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p2 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
ovs-appctl netdev-dummy/receive p2 "in_port(1),packet_type(ns=0,id=0),eth(src=50:54:00:00:00:0a,dst=50:54:00:00:00:0b),eth_type(0x0800),ipv4(src=$ADDR1,dst=$ADDRD,proto=1,tos=0,ttl=64,frag=no),icmp(type=8,code=0)" --len 60
# with burst size 2 (== 2000 bits). 4 packets, (240 bytes == 1920 bits) pass, but the last packet should hit the drop band.
(ovs-ofctl -O OpenFlow13 meter-stats br0 | strip_timers) > meterstats.txt
cat <<EOF > meterstats_expected.txt
OFPST_METER reply (OF1.3) (xid=0x2):
meter:2 flow_count:1 packet_in_count:5 byte_in_count:300 duration:0.0s bands:
0: packet_count:1 byte_count:60
diff --ignore-all-space meterstats_expected.txt meterstats.txt
if test $? -ne 0; then
    exit 14
exit $exitret
z3test drain combination failed: shell return code:%dr
test_drain_combinationO
1z TestDrain.test_drain_combination)
__name__
__module__
__qualname__r
__classcell__r
__main__)
__doc__Z
unittestZ
na_tests.common.utilityr
na_tests.commonr
TestCaseBaser
mainr
<module>
